title: Courgette and Bacon Fritters
description: Golden savoury fritters made with courgette, bacon, ricotta and herbs, perfect for brunch or a light meal.
steps: Heat a little olive oil in a large non-stick frying pan and cook the bacon until golden and crisp. Set aside.

Put the flour into a large bowl, then mix in the ricotta, milk and eggs to make a smooth batter. Lift handfuls of the grated courgette over the sink and squeeze as hard as you can to remove excess moisture, then stir it into the batter.
Stir in the broccoli, chives and cooked bacon. Season well with salt.

Wipe out the bacon pan, add a little olive oil and return to a low–medium heat. Add large spoonfuls of the batter, spreading slightly to level. Cook in batches for about 4 minutes until golden on the bottom, then flip and cook for a further 4 minutes until both sides are golden.
Lift onto a plate, cover with foil and keep warm while you cook the rest of the batter.
If desired, poach eggs to serve on top. Serve with a green salad.

To freeze ahead: Cool completely, stack with baking parchment between layers and freeze in a freezerproof container for up to 2 months. Reheat in a single layer on baking sheets in an oven preheated to 200°C (180°C fan) for 15–20 minutes until piping hot.
